trans-unit.source: Understanding the Print Layout Composer
trans-unit.target: Understanding the Print Layout Composer

trans-unit.source: The interface of the Print Layout Composer. :::
trans-unit.target: The interface of the Print Layout Composer. :::

trans-unit.source: __Layout Settings__ (Add pages, Export Map, manage panels)
trans-unit.target: __Layout Settings__ (Add pages, Export Map, manage panels)

trans-unit.source: __Dialer Tools__ (Save, New, Duplicate, Add items from template, Save template)
trans-unit.target: __Dialer Tools__ (Save, New, Duplicate, Add items from template, Save template)

trans-unit.source: __Navigation bar__ (Zoom, Refresh, Lock/Unlock elements)
trans-unit.target: __Navigation bar__ (Zoom, Refresh, Lock/Unlock elements)

trans-unit.source: __Toolbar__ (Zoom, Select, Move in Map, Add new map/image/text/legend/scale/shape/...)
trans-unit.target: __Toolbar__ (Zoom, Select, Move in Map, Add new map/image/text/legend/scale/shape/...)

trans-unit.source: __Feature Panel__: Displays the elements added to the print layout
trans-unit.target: __Feature Panel__: Displays the elements added to the print layout

trans-unit.source: __Advanced Options__: Customise each element of the layer
trans-unit.target: __Advanced Options__: Customise each element of the layer

trans-unit.source: The print layout manager also works with toolbars (on the left side of the screen), where you can select different tools (e.g. to add images) and panels (on the right side of the screen), where you can change the settings of the tools or the elements you have added to the map.
trans-unit.target: The print layout manager also works with toolbars (on the left side of the screen), where you can select different tools (e.g. to add images) and panels (on the right side of the screen), where you can change the settings of the tools or the elements you have added to the map.

trans-unit.source: First of all, you should always set the size of your map:
trans-unit.target: First of all, you should always set the size of your map:

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the blank map > `Page Properties`.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the blank map > `Page Properties`.

trans-unit.source: Choose __the size of your document__ (A4, A3, A2). A4 and A3 are the most commonly used sizes for maps.
trans-unit.target: Choose __the size of your document__ (A4, A3, A2). A4 and A3 are the most commonly used sizes for maps.

trans-unit.source: Choose the orientation (Landscape or Portrait).
trans-unit.target: Choose the orientation (Landscape or Portrait).

trans-unit.source: Adding Elements to the Print Layout
trans-unit.target: Adding Elements to the Print Layout

trans-unit.source: Adding a New Map
trans-unit.target: Adding a New Map

trans-unit.source: Add a new map by clicking on the ![](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_insert_map_icon.png) `Add map`-button on the __toolbar on the left__ and drag a rectangle on the map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Add a new map by clicking on the ![](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_insert_map_icon.png) `Add map`-button on the __toolbar on the left__ and drag a rectangle on the map canvas.

trans-unit.source: To move the map on the canvas, simply __select the map__ and __drag__ it with your mouse.
trans-unit.target: To move the map on the canvas, simply __select the map__ and __drag__ it with your mouse.

trans-unit.source: To move within a map select ![](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_move_content_icon.png) `Move item content`-button on the left toolbar.
trans-unit.target: To move within a map select ![](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_move_content_icon.png) `Move item content`-button on the left toolbar.

trans-unit.source: To zoom in on the map, while using the ![](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_move_content_icon.png) `Move item content` tool, you can __Press CTRL + scroll the mouse wheel__ (gently) or enter the scale manually in the item properties.
trans-unit.target: To zoom in on the map, while using the ![](/fig/30.30.2_print_layout_move_content_icon.png) `Move item content` tool, you can __Press CTRL + scroll the mouse wheel__ (gently) or enter the scale manually in the item properties.

trans-unit.source: Adding a new map to the Print Layout (Source: CartONG). :::
trans-unit.target: Adding a new map to the Print Layout (Source: CartONG). :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-set} :::{tab-item} Adding a new map
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-set} :::{tab-item} Adding a new map

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Moving and scaling the map
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Moving and scaling the map

trans-unit.source: Adding a Title or a Text Box
trans-unit.target: Adding a Title or a Text Box

trans-unit.source: A title should describe the phenomenon represented on the map.
trans-unit.target: A title should describe the phenomenon represented on the map.

trans-unit.source: To add text (title, explanations), use the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_text.png) `Add Label`-tool and draw a rectangle of the desired size.
trans-unit.target: To add text (title, explanations), use the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_text.png) `Add Label`-tool and draw a rectangle of the desired size.

trans-unit.source: In the __Item Properties__ panel (on the right of your screen) you can __enter your text__ and __change the font, style, colour, etc.__ (Remember to use the scroll bar in the window to see all the options).
trans-unit.target: In the __Item Properties__ panel (on the right of your screen) you can __enter your text__ and __change the font, style, colour, etc.__ (Remember to use the scroll bar in the window to see all the options).

trans-unit.source: Adding text to the print layout (Source: CartONG). :::
trans-unit.target: Adding text to the print layout (Source: CartONG). :::

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Video: Adding a textbox
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Video: Adding a textbox

trans-unit.source: Adding an Image or Logo
trans-unit.target: Adding an Image or Logo

trans-unit.source: If you are working for an organisation, most likely you will add the logo of that organization on the maps you produce.
trans-unit.target: If you are working for an organisation, most likely you will add the logo of that organization on the maps you produce.

trans-unit.source: Click on the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_image.png) `Add image`-tool in the left toolbar.
trans-unit.target: Click on the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_image.png) `Add image`-tool in the left toolbar.

trans-unit.source: Drag a rectangle on the canvas.
trans-unit.target: Drag a rectangle on the canvas.

trans-unit.source: In the __Item Properties__ tab, you will have the option to choose an SVG image from your SVG-library in QGIS or choose a __Raster image__. Most image files are raster images.
trans-unit.target: In the __Item Properties__ tab, you will have the option to choose an SVG image from your SVG-library in QGIS or choose a __Raster image__. Most image files are raster images.

trans-unit.source: Select `Raster image` and click on `...` to choose the location of the image.
trans-unit.target: Select `Raster image` and click on `...` to choose the location of the image.

trans-unit.source: Your image will appear in the print layout. In order to make sure that the image does not get distorted, leave the `Resize Mode` on "Zoom".
trans-unit.target: Your image will appear in the print layout. In order to make sure that the image does not get distorted, leave the `Resize Mode` on "Zoom".

trans-unit.source: Adding an image or logo to the print layout. :::
trans-unit.target: Adding an image or logo to the print layout. :::

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Adding an image to the print layout
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Adding an image to the print layout
:::

trans-unit.source: Adding a Legend
trans-unit.target: Adding a Legend

trans-unit.source: Before adding a legend, make sure that:
trans-unit.target: Before adding a legend, make sure that:

trans-unit.source: All your layers have an explicit name ("rivers", "primary roads",...).
trans-unit.target: All your layers have an explicit name ("rivers", "primary roads",...).

trans-unit.source: You use the final version of your map (no more layers to add, move, rename or modify). You can still modify them later but you will have to redo the legend.
trans-unit.target: You use the final version of your map (no more layers to add, move, rename or modify). You can still modify them later but you will have to redo the legend.

trans-unit.source: To add a legend, you can use the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_legend.png) `Add legend`-button on the __left toolbar__.
trans-unit.target: To add a legend, you can use the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_add_legend.png) `Add legend`-button on the __left toolbar__.

trans-unit.source: Adding a legend to the print layout. :::
trans-unit.target: Adding a legend to the print layout. :::

trans-unit.source: In the __Item Properties__ panel, if you keep the __`Auto update`__ option checked, new layers added to your project will automatically be added to the legend but you cannot control them individually (rename if necessary, reorder or remove items). Once the option is unchecked, you can update the name of the layers, group them, remove or reorganise them, etc.
trans-unit.target: In the __Item Properties__ panel, if you keep the __`Auto update`__ option checked, new layers added to your project will automatically be added to the legend but you cannot control them individually (rename if necessary, reorder or remove items). Once the option is unchecked, you can update the name of the layers, group them, remove or reorganise them, etc.

trans-unit.source: If you have too many items on your legend, and they don't fit on your map horizontally, you can also split the legend into several columns by navigating through the Item Properties panel, expand the `Columns`-section, and increase the `Count`.
trans-unit.target: If you have too many items on your legend, and they don't fit on your map horizontally, you can also split the legend into several columns by navigating through the Item Properties panel, expand the `Columns`-section, and increase the `Count`.

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-set} :::{tab-item} Adding a legend
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-set} :::{tab-item} Adding a legend

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Editing the legend
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Editing the legend

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Adding a two-column legend
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Adding a two-column legend

trans-unit.source: Sometimes the space on your map is not suited for a single vertical legend. In this case, it is useful to add multiple columns. Make sure to group the legend items logically so that the vertical legend is easier to read.
trans-unit.target: Sometimes the space on your map is not suited for a single vertical legend. In this case, it is useful to add multiple columns. Make sure to group the legend items logically so that the vertical legend is easier to read.

trans-unit.source: Adding a Scale Bar
trans-unit.target: Adding a Scale Bar

trans-unit.source: Before adding a scale bar, select your main map and check in the __Item Properties__ panel that the `Scale` field has a __round number__ .
trans-unit.target: Before adding a scale bar, select your main map and check in the __Item Properties__ panel that the `Scale` field has a __round number__ .

trans-unit.source: Make sure that the scale is at a round number. :::
trans-unit.target: Make sure that the scale is at a round number. :::

trans-unit.source: To add a scale bar, you can use the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_scale_bar.png)`Add scale bar`-button on the __left toolbar__. In the __Item Properties__ panel, customize the following functions.
trans-unit.target: To add a scale bar, you can use the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_scale_bar.png)`Add scale bar`-button on the __left toolbar__. In the __Item Properties__ panel, customize the following functions.

trans-unit.source: Which Map __is related to the scale__.
trans-unit.target: Which Map __is related to the scale__.

trans-unit.source: __Unit system of the bar__ (metres, miles, degrees).
trans-unit.target: __Unit system of the bar__ (metres, miles, degrees).

trans-unit.source: __Segments on the left__: segments shown before 0 m (always set to 0).
trans-unit.target: __Segments on the left__: segments shown before 0 m (always set to 0).

trans-unit.source: __Fixed width__: define the width of each segment (e.g. 1 km, 10 km, 50 km, ...).
trans-unit.target: __Fixed width__: define the width of each segment (e.g. 1 km, 10 km, 50 km, ...).

trans-unit.source: __Height__: height (thickness) of the scale bar.
trans-unit.target: __Height__: height (thickness) of the scale bar.

trans-unit.source: There are many other options to customize the scale bar (change the font, colours, etc.).
trans-unit.target: There are many other options to customize the scale bar (change the font, colours, etc.).

trans-unit.source: Adding and customising the scale bar. :::
trans-unit.target: Adding and customising the scale bar. :::

trans-unit.source: Adding an Overview Map
trans-unit.target: Adding an Overview Map

trans-unit.source: Adding an overview map in the corner of your map will help locate the area you are viewing on the main map.
trans-unit.target: Adding an overview map in the corner of your map will help locate the area you are viewing on the main map.

trans-unit.source: To create an overview map, you need to follow these steps:
trans-unit.target: To create an overview map, you need to follow these steps:

trans-unit.source: Make sure that you __lock the layers and layer styling__ for your main map.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that you __lock the layers and layer styling__ for your main map.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the __item properties__ panel > `Lock layers` and `Lock styles for layers`.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the __item properties__ panel > `Lock layers` and `Lock styles for layers`.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a __layer with national or subnational borders or important landmarks__ in your project (e.g: Administrative boundaries, Capitals). These should not be the same layers as your main map. If necessary, you can duplicate layers you wish to use on the overview map (such as administrative boundaries). Do not change the layers of your main map, if you intend to change the symbology later on.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a __layer with national or subnational borders or important landmarks__ in your project (e.g: Administrative boundaries, Capitals). These should not be the same layers as your main map. If necessary, you can duplicate layers you wish to use on the overview map (such as administrative boundaries). Do not change the layers of your main map, if you intend to change the symbology later on.

trans-unit.source: __Insert the overview map__ into your print layout, by using the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_insert_map_icon.png)`Add Map` tool (in the bottom right corner, for example).
trans-unit.target: __Insert the overview map__ into your print layout, by using the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_insert_map_icon.png)`Add Map` tool (in the bottom right corner, for example).

trans-unit.source: __Lock the new map__ in the Item properties panel.
trans-unit.target: __Lock the new map__ in the Item properties panel.

trans-unit.source: Add a rectangle to display the extent of your main map.
trans-unit.target: Add a rectangle to display the extent of your main map.

trans-unit.source: Go to the __properties__ of your Main map > scroll down until you see __"Overviews"__.
trans-unit.target: Go to the __properties__ of your Main map > scroll down until you see __"Overviews"__.

trans-unit.source: Add an Overview by clicking on the `+`-button.
trans-unit.target: Add an Overview by clicking on the `+`-button.

trans-unit.source: __Link the main map__ by selecting it in the `Map frame` option.
trans-unit.target: __Link the main map__ by selecting it in the `Map frame` option.

trans-unit.source: An overview map should show important landmarks and borders so the reader is able to locate the region shown on the map without having specific knowledge of the region. :::
trans-unit.target: An overview map should show important landmarks and borders so the reader is able to locate the region shown on the map without having specific knowledge of the region. :::

trans-unit.source: Add an overview map and __lock the layer__. :::
trans-unit.target: Add an overview map and __lock the layer__. :::

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Setting up an overview map
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Setting up an overview map
:::

trans-unit.source: Exporting the print layout
trans-unit.target: Exporting the print layout

trans-unit.source: Once you are finished with the map composition, it is time to export the print layout as a PDF or SVG file.
trans-unit.target: Once you are finished with the map composition, it is time to export the print layout as a PDF or SVG file.

trans-unit.source: In the toolbar above the canvas, click on the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_export_pdf.png) `Export as PDF`-button.
trans-unit.target: In the toolbar above the canvas, click on the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_export_pdf.png) `Export as PDF`-button.

trans-unit.source: Give the new file a name and select the location you want to save it.
trans-unit.target: Give the new file a name and select the location you want to save it.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Save`.

trans-unit.source: A new window "PDF Export Options" will open. Here you can adjust the compression algorithm. For the best results, select the lossless image compression.
trans-unit.target: A new window "PDF Export Options" will open. Here you can adjust the compression algorithm. For the best results, select the lossless image compression.

trans-unit.source: Click `Save` again.
trans-unit.target: Click `Save` again.

trans-unit.source: A new green bar will pop up underneath the toolbars. Click on the file link to __review the exported map__.
trans-unit.target: A new green bar will pop up underneath the toolbars. Click on the file link to __review the exported map__.

trans-unit.source: Map Templates
trans-unit.target: Map Templates

trans-unit.source: Map templates can simplify and speed up the creation of a print layout by saving the arrangement of elements. However, they do not save the project's layers and images, which will need to be reconfigured each time. If you work for an organisation that frequently publishes maps, or you need to create several maps on the same topics but in different regions or times, you can use map templates to skip the arrangement of elements.
trans-unit.target: Map templates can simplify and speed up the creation of a print layout by saving the arrangement of elements. However, they do not save the project's layers and images, which will need to be reconfigured each time. If you work for an organisation that frequently publishes maps, or you need to create several maps on the same topics but in different regions or times, you can use map templates to skip the arrangement of elements.

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-set} :::{tab-item} Saving a template
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-set} :::{tab-item} Saving a template

trans-unit.source: Once you are satisfied with your map layout, click on the ![](../../fig/en_30.30.2_save_as_template.png) `Save as template`-button in the toolbar above the canvas to save it as a new template.
trans-unit.target: Once you are satisfied with your map layout, click on the ![](../../fig/en_30.30.2_save_as_template.png) `Save as template`-button in the toolbar above the canvas to save it as a new template.

trans-unit.source: Choose a location where you want to save the template. Ideally, you should choose the template directory.
trans-unit.target: Choose a location where you want to save the template. Ideally, you should choose the template directory.

trans-unit.source: Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: You can open the template by dragging it into a QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: You can open the template by dragging it into a QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Opening a template
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Opening a template

trans-unit.source: You can drag and drop template-files (`.qpt`, QGIS template file) into QGIS or use the __Layout manager__.
trans-unit.target: You can drag and drop template-files (`.qpt`, QGIS template file) into QGIS or use the __Layout manager__.

trans-unit.source: Open the Layout manager under `Layout` → `Layout Manager`.
trans-unit.target: Open the Layout manager under `Layout` → `Layout Manager`.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the section "__New from Template__".
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the section "__New from Template__".

trans-unit.source: Choose `Specific` and select the location where you saved your template.
trans-unit.target: Choose `Specific` and select the location where you saved your template.

trans-unit.source: Click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Template directory
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Template directory

trans-unit.source: The template directory is where QGIS stores layout templates. If you have templates saved here, you can load templates directly through the layout manager without selecting the file. On Windows computers, the file path is `\Users\AppData\Roaming\QGIS\QGIS3\profiles\default\composer_templates`.
trans-unit.target: The template directory is where QGIS stores layout templates. If you have templates saved here, you can load templates directly through the layout manager without selecting the file. On Windows computers, the file path is `\Users\AppData\Roaming\QGIS\QGIS3\profiles\default\composer_templates`.

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} The layout manager in QGIS already has a dedicated location for map templates. On Windows computers, the file path is `\Users\AppData\Roaming\QGIS\QGIS3\profiles\default\composer_templates`. If you save templates here, you can load templates directly through the layout manager without looking for the file. You can also add file paths in the QGIS template settings. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} The layout manager in QGIS already has a dedicated location for map templates. On Windows computers, the file path is `\Users\AppData\Roaming\QGIS\QGIS3\profiles\default\composer_templates`. If you save templates here, you can load templates directly through the layout manager without looking for the file. You can also add file paths in the QGIS template settings. :::

trans-unit.source: The Atlas Function (Automatic Map Generation)
trans-unit.target: The Atlas Function (Automatic Map Generation)

trans-unit.source: In some cases, it can be necessary to create multiple maps for different locations with the same layers. For example, if you have a detailed dataset on affected flood areas in Nigeria, you can create a more detailed map for each subnational district. In QGIS, this can be done automatically with the __Atlas Function__.
trans-unit.target: In some cases, it can be necessary to create multiple maps for different locations with the same layers. For example, if you have a detailed dataset on affected flood areas in Nigeria, you can create a more detailed map for each subnational district. In QGIS, this can be done automatically with the __Atlas Function__.

trans-unit.source: The Atlas Function can be found in the __Print Layout Composer__ on the toolbar.
trans-unit.target: The Atlas Function can be found in the __Print Layout Composer__ on the toolbar.

trans-unit.source: The atlas toolbar. :::
trans-unit.target: The atlas toolbar. :::

trans-unit.source: Generating an Atlas
trans-unit.target: Generating an Atlas

trans-unit.source: An atlas will generate a new page with the same map layout for each feature in a layer. For most purposes, it is useful to first create a map layout with the elements such as legend, sources and overview map and then insert the map item that will be controlled by the Atlas. To generate an atlas:
trans-unit.target: An atlas will generate a new page with the same map layout for each feature in a layer. For most purposes, it is useful to first create a map layout with the elements such as legend, sources and overview map and then insert the map item that will be controlled by the Atlas. To generate an atlas:

trans-unit.source: Click on the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_atlas_settings.png) `Atlas Settings`-button in the Atlas Toolbar.
trans-unit.target: Click on the ![](../../fig/30.30.2_print_layout_atlas_settings.png) `Atlas Settings`-button in the Atlas Toolbar.

trans-unit.source: In the new window, activate the `Generate an Atlas` option.
trans-unit.target: In the new window, activate the `Generate an Atlas` option.

trans-unit.source: Select the `Coverage Layer`. This will determine the features or polygons that will be displayed on a page. In our example, we will use the subnational administrative regions in Nigeria (`ADM1`).
trans-unit.target: Select the `Coverage Layer`. This will determine the features or polygons that will be displayed on a page. In our example, we will use the subnational administrative regions in Nigeria (`ADM1`).

trans-unit.source: Select the `Page Name`. This should be the name of the subnational region or location that is displayed on that page. To display the name of the region, we will choose the column `ADM1_REF`, which holds the names of the regions in English.
trans-unit.target: Select the `Page Name`. This should be the name of the subnational region or location that is displayed on that page. To display the name of the region, we will choose the column `ADM1_REF`, which holds the names of the regions in English.

trans-unit.source: Now let's add a map to the empty print layout.
trans-unit.target: Now let's add a map to the empty print layout.

trans-unit.source: Click on the map and navigate to the __Layer Properties__ window on the right.
trans-unit.target: Click on the map and navigate to the __Layer Properties__ window on the right.

trans-unit.source: Scroll down until you see the option `Controlled by Atlas` and activate it.
trans-unit.target: Scroll down until you see the option `Controlled by Atlas` and activate it.

trans-unit.source: Now activate the preview of the Atlas in the __Atlas Toolbar__. Otherwise, the print layout will not update to show you the atlas page. You can click through each page to see how it looks. Depending on the amount of features on your map, they may take a while to render.
trans-unit.target: Now activate the preview of the Atlas in the __Atlas Toolbar__. Otherwise, the print layout will not update to show you the atlas page. You can click through each page to see how it looks. Depending on the amount of features on your map, they may take a while to render.

trans-unit.source: Now you can adjust the __Margin options__ to best fit the readability of the map. By default, it is set to 10% and this should fit most purposes.
trans-unit.target: Now you can adjust the __Margin options__ to best fit the readability of the map. By default, it is set to 10% and this should fit most purposes.

trans-unit.source: Before printing or exporting the atlas, make sure to check every page that other elements of the map do not cover the represented region.
trans-unit.target: Before printing or exporting the atlas, make sure to check every page that other elements of the map do not cover the represented region.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Setting up an Atlas
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Setting up an Atlas
:::

trans-unit.source: Self-Assessment Questions
trans-unit.target: Self-Assessment Questions

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Test your knowledge :class: note
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Test your knowledge :class: note

trans-unit.source: __How do you create or open a layout in QGIS?__
trans-unit.target: __How do you create or open a layout in QGIS?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: In QGIS, you go to `Project` → `New Print Layout` to create a new layout.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS, you go to `Project` → `New Print Layout` to create a new layout.

trans-unit.source: That opens the Print Layout Composer, a new window where you can begin composing your map layout.
trans-unit.target: That opens the Print Layout Composer, a new window where you can begin composing your map layout.

trans-unit.source: If you already have layouts saved __in this project__, you can open them via the __Layout Manager__ (`Project` → `Layout Manager`), where you will find a list of existing layouts.
trans-unit.target: If you already have layouts saved __in this project__, you can open them via the __Layout Manager__ (`Project` → `Layout Manager`), where you will find a list of existing layouts.

trans-unit.source: Additionally, you can create a layout from a template file via the Layout Manager (__"New from template"__) if you have saved a `.qpt` file. :::
trans-unit.target: Additionally, you can create a layout from a template file via the Layout Manager (__"New from template"__) if you have saved a `.qpt` file. :::

trans-unit.source: __What is the difference between the “map item” (map frame) inside a layout and the main QGIS map canvas?__
trans-unit.target: __What is the difference between the “map item” (map frame) inside a layout and the main QGIS map canvas?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: The map item in the print layout composer is a of the map canvas in the main QGIS window and shows the layers as they are shown in the main window.
trans-unit.target: The map item in the print layout composer is a of the map canvas in the main QGIS window and shows the layers as they are shown in the main window.

trans-unit.source: In the main map canvas, you are able to interact with the data whereas you can only see the data in the print layout composer.
trans-unit.target: In the main map canvas, you are able to interact with the data whereas you can only see the data in the print layout composer.

trans-unit.source: The map item shows the map as it is in the map canvas (you might have to update the map item to show changed symbology), unless you lock the layers and layers styles for the specific map item. ::
trans-unit.target: The map item shows the map as it is in the map canvas (you might have to update the map item to show changed symbology), unless you lock the layers and layers styles for the specific map item. ::

trans-unit.source: __How do you lock layer in a map you've added to the print layout?__
trans-unit.target: __How do you lock layer in a map you've added to the print layout?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer Locking is essential if you want the layout’s map item to preserve exactly what you set, even if later you change things in the project.
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer Locking is essential if you want the layout’s map item to preserve exactly what you set, even if later you change things in the project.

trans-unit.source: Select the map item in the print layout composer
trans-unit.target: Select the map item in the print layout composer

trans-unit.source: In its __Item properties panel__, there is an option to __lock layers and styling__ for that map.
trans-unit.target: In its __Item properties panel__, there is an option to __lock layers and styling__ for that map.

trans-unit.source: Once locked, you can change the layer order or layer symbology in the main QGIS window without changing the map in the print layout.
trans-unit.target: Once locked, you can change the layer order or layer symbology in the main QGIS window without changing the map in the print layout.
